rolling_30d.summary: CORT Rental's local pages site generated 81,957 sessions over the last 30 days, representing a strong YoY surge of +302.7% despite a MoM decline of -10.5%. Engagement quality improved significantly, with engaged sessions climbing +3.6% MoM and +42.2% YoY, while engagement rate expanded 2.5 percentage points MoM—though it contracted 33.7 pp YoY, likely due to higher overall traffic volume diluting the rate. Direct traffic dominates at 76.4% of sessions (+1440.6% YoY), suggesting strong brand awareness and repeat visits, though it declined 16.9% MoM, signaling potential seasonal softness or competitive pressure. Paid Search grew 12.4% MoM and 470% YoY, indicating effective paid acquisition strategies. High-value conversion events show mixed momentum: calls surged +18.1% MoM (+16.3% YoY), while rent_for_home events climbed +8.6% MoM but fell -38.1% YoY—a red flag for product-level demand. Location-finding intent remains robust, with store_locate and locate_me combining for 2,796 events and strong YoY growth of 109.4% and 896.4% respectively. YTD 2026 traffic stands at 244,043 sessions, a +279.1% increase versus YTD 2025, with January–March showing consistent triple-digit MoM growth, positioning the brand for a record year.

quick_wins.title: Phone calls momentum accelerates
quick_wins.detail: Calls events increased +18.1% MoM to 293 total, the strongest sequential lift among high-value conversions. Paired with +16.3% YoY growth, this signals improving conversion quality and warrants continued investment in call-to-action placement.

quick_wins.title: Paid Search ROI expanding
quick_wins.detail: Paid Search sessions grew +12.4% MoM and +470% YoY, capturing 8,350 sessions (10.2% of traffic). This channel is outpacing organic and referral, suggesting keyword and creative optimization is yielding returns.

quick_wins.title: Homepage engagement driving traffic
quick_wins.detail: The homepage (/) generated 4,178 sessions with a 42.6% engagement rate, up +31.4% MoM and +208.6% YoY. This is the top landing page and shows strong momentum, indicating effective brand messaging.

quick_wins.title: Location pages showing strong engagement
quick_wins.detail: Regional pages like /texas/houston (61.1% engagement), /new-york/new-york/140-e-45th-st (65.7%), and /texas/austin (57.9%) are outperforming site average, with robust YoY growth (+43.2%, +64.4%, +35.8% respectively). These pages are winning high-intent visitors.

potential_warnings.title: Sessions declining month-over-month
potential_warnings.detail: Despite strong YoY growth, sessions fell -10.5% MoM to 81,957. This reverses the upward trajectory seen across Q1 2026 and warrants investigation into seasonal patterns, competitive activity, or paid media budget allocation.

potential_warnings.title: rent_for_home product interest collapsing YoY
potential_warnings.detail: rent_for_home events dropped -38.1% YoY while climbing +8.6% MoM, suggesting recent campaign recovery masking a structural demand decline. This product line is losing appeal compared to the prior year and may need repositioning or competitive analysis.

potential_warnings.title: Organic Search traffic declining YoY
potential_warnings.detail: Organic Search fell -29.9% YoY (9,704 sessions) while growing +6.5% MoM. SEO performance is lagging year-over-year, potentially indicating keyword ranking losses or increased SERP competition that offset recent recovery efforts.

potential_warnings.title: Organic Social engagement collapsing
potential_warnings.detail: Organic Social sessions plummeted -41.0% MoM and -53.2% YoY to just 36 sessions. This channel is negligible and declining sharply, suggesting underinvestment, algorithm changes, or ineffective content strategy.

biggest_opportunities.title: Expand location-finder intent capture
biggest_opportunities.detail: store_locate (1,401 events) and locate_me (1,395 events) are thriving with +109.4% and +896.4% YoY growth respectively. Doubling down on location-based targeting, map integrations, and local inventory ads could capitalize on this high-intent behavior.

biggest_opportunities.title: Optimize Paid Search spend allocation
biggest_opportunities.detail: Paid Search is the only organic-comparable channel growing faster YoY (+470%) than overall traffic (+302.7%), and it's the second-largest driver at 10.2% of sessions. Increasing budget share from Direct and Organic Search into Paid Search keywords could accelerate growth.

biggest_opportunities.title: Reverse rent_for_home decline with urgency/scarcity campaigns
biggest_opportunities.detail: rent_for_home is down -38.1% YoY despite recent MoM recovery, indicating lost market share. Introducing limited-time offers, scarcity messaging, or product bundling on landing pages and paid ads could reignite demand.

biggest_opportunities.title: Double down on regional landing page optimization
biggest_opportunities.detail: High-engagement location pages like /texas/houston (61.1%), /new-york/new-york/140-e-45th-st (65.7%), and /illinois/chicago (43.7% → +57.1% MoM) are outperforming site average. Replicating their conversion elements—localized CTAs, nearby inventory, live availability—across underperforming regional pages could lift overall funnel.

biggest_opportunities.title: Rebuild Organic Search presence
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic Search is down -29.9% YoY and represents only 11.8% of traffic. A content audit targeting high-intent keywords (e.g., 'rent furniture [city]', 'temporary office solutions') and backlink building could recover lost visibility without incremental paid spend.

last_calendar_month.summary: CORT Rental demonstrated robust YoY growth in March 2026, with sessions up 314.0% YoY to 86,606, though MoM momentum flatlined at -0.1%. More encouraging: Engaged Sessions grew 13.7% MoM and 44.5% YoY to 15,587, signaling stronger quality despite marginal session decline. Engagement Rate improved to 18.0% (+2.2 pp MoM), a notable positive given YoY compression of 33.6 pp—likely due to channel mix shift. Direct traffic dominates at 76.4% of sessions with explosive 1458.0% YoY growth, while Paid Search surged 26.2% MoM and 508.9% YoY, indicating effective paid acquisition scaling. High-value conversions showed mixed signals: Calls rose 33.3% MoM (+19.8% YoY) to 308 events, but rent_for_home declined 37.6% YoY despite +21.9% MoM gain. Location-intent events thrived—locate_me jumped 878.7% YoY and store_locate grew 13.3% MoM—reflecting strong mobile and local search behavior. Homepage (/) captured 4,278 sessions with 42.3% engagement and +39.7% MoM growth; regional pages like /texas/houston (60.3% engagement, +45.5% MoM) and /new-york (+75.7% MoM) outperformed with higher intent. Q1 2026 cumulative sessions reached 244,043 (+279.1% YoY), establishing a strong seasonal baseline for the year. Primary headwind: organic search declined 28.7% YoY despite modest +19.2% MoM recovery, and rent_for_office dropped 8.8% MoM, warranting tactical attention.

quick_wins.title: Calls conversion surge
quick_wins.detail: Phone call initiations grew 33.3% MoM to 308 events, a high-value conversion signal gaining momentum heading into Q2.

quick_wins.title: Location intent acceleration
quick_wins.detail: Locate_me events exploded 878.7% YoY to 1,468; paired with store_locate growth of 13.3% MoM, users are actively seeking local inventory—prime upsell moment.

quick_wins.title: Regional page engagement wins
quick_wins.detail: /texas/houston achieved 60.3% engagement (+45.5% MoM) and /new-york posted 75.7% MoM growth; these localized pages are outperforming the homepage and warrant increased paid/organic spend.

quick_wins.title: Paid Search momentum
quick_wins.detail: Paid Search sessions grew 26.2% MoM and 508.9% YoY to 8,738, outpacing organic search recovery; continued investment is clearly driving high-intent traffic.

potential_warnings.title: Organic Search erosion
potential_warnings.detail: Organic Search declined 28.7% YoY to 10,086 sessions (11.6% of mix), signaling keyword visibility loss or increased competitive pressure; modest +19.2% MoM recovery is insufficient to offset annual decay.

potential_warnings.title: Rent-for-home conversion cliff
potential_warnings.detail: rent_for_home events fell 37.6% YoY despite +21.9% MoM uptick to 3,729 events; this high-intent event is underperforming relative to traffic gains, suggesting messaging or targeting misalignment on home rental segments.

potential_warnings.title: Organic Social collapse
potential_warnings.detail: Organic Social traffic plummeted 27.3% MoM and 51.2% YoY to just 40 sessions; either social strategy has stalled or platform algorithm changes have reduced reach.

potential_warnings.title: Session growth deceleration MoM
potential_warnings.detail: Despite 314.0% YoY growth, March sessions were essentially flat MoM (-0.1%), indicating potential saturation in current acquisition channels or seasonal demand softening heading into April.

biggest_opportunities.title: Expand regional paid + organic targeting
biggest_opportunities.detail: /texas/houston (60.3% engagement, 390 sessions), /new-york (75.7% MoM growth), and /illinois/chicago (69.4% MoM growth) are regional hotspots with disproportionately high engagement; allocate incremental budget to these geo-segments.

biggest_opportunities.title: Recover Organic Search visibility
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic Search is the only major channel in YoY decline (-28.7% to 10,086 sessions); conduct keyword audits on high-performing regional pages and rebuild rankings for home/office rental categories.

biggest_opportunities.title: Monetize location-intent traffic
biggest_opportunities.detail: Locate_me (1,468 events, +878.7% YoY) and store_locate (1,447 events) users are primed for conversion; test in-page booking CTAs and location-based promos to drive rent_for_home and calls higher.

biggest_opportunities.title: Investigate rent_for_office decline
biggest_opportunities.detail: rent_for_office dropped 8.8% MoM to 239 events despite broader engagement gains; audit the office rental funnel and messaging to identify friction points or messaging gaps.

biggest_opportunities.title: Resurrect Organic Social strategy
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic Social collapsed 51.2% YoY to 40 sessions; evaluate platform mix, content strategy, and posting cadence; even modest social recovery could diversify acquisition risk away from paid channels.
